category_title: Students with externalizing behaviors and low frustration tolerance
minimal_context: The student is playing a board game involving rolling dice and turn-taking at a table during a nurture group session.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is your turn to roll the dice for the board game.

STUDENT: I want to win. I don't want to be losing.

TUTOR: Go ahead and roll the dice to see what number you get.

STUDENT: (Rolls the dice, begins to sulk and get cross) I'm losing the game.

TUTOR: You did not roll the correct number. It is the next person's turn.

STUDENT: I didn't roll the correct number! (Throws things across the table and stamps off)

options.A: Instruct the children to play anything with any material available in the room.
options.B: Facilitate a discussion about the student's feelings and behavior.
options.C: Say 'Whose turn?' as a verbal prompt for the board game.
options.D: Alter the original game plans and incorporate the student's contributions.
